Building Inclusive Futures

Next Generation Leaders Award 2022

MD Tahidul Islam Masum is a youth development practitioner, educator, climate advocate, and tech innovator with over a decade of experience working at the intersection of sustainability, community development, and digital transformation.

He currently serves as an Adjunct Faculty Member and Coordinator of International Affairs at Bandarban University, where he focuses on teaching, mentoring, and strengthening the research, innovation, and technical capacity of young people — particularly from underrepresented and remote communities in the Chittagong Hill Tracts.

He serves as a Board Member of Green Lead, a Constituency Member of UNEP, and a Global Shaper of the WEF — contributing to global discussions on environmental governance, youth policy advocacy, sustainability, and inclusive development.

He is deeply committed to building inclusive, resilient, and future-ready ecosystems where young people can thrive through education, technology, climate resilience, and ethical leadership.

Event Photo

Event Details

Cultural & Environmental Dialogue

Masum represented Bangladesh at the International Global Network Forum, themed "The Evolving of Cultural and Environmental Awareness for a Brighter Future." The event brought together young leaders from across Asia and Africa to engage in cross-cultural dialogue on environmental sustainability, cultural identity, and youth-led solutions for a shared global future.

🇧🇩 Bangladesh 🇱🇰 Sri Lanka 🇮🇳 India 🇵🇭 Philippines 🇿🇦 South Africa

About the Initiative
🌐
World Economic Forum Global Shapers Community
📍
Chattogram Hub, Bangladesh
🎓
Skill Hub for Chattogram City Corporation students
💻
Focus: Globally relevant & cutting-edge technologies
Activity Details

Global Shapers Hold
'Meet the Leader'
with Chattogram Mayor

The Global Shapers Community, a youth initiative created by the World Economic Forum, paid a courtesy call on the Honorable Mayor of Chattogram at his office.

During the meeting, discussions focused on the potential establishment of a Skill Hub, with the support of the Global Shapers, aimed at enabling students from schools under the Chattogram City Corporation to develop skills in globally relevant and cutting-edge technologies.

The discussion was held as part of the Global Shapers' global flagship initiative, "Meet the Leader."

The Honorable Mayor also shared insights into his ongoing initiatives and future plans for youth development in Chattogram and expressed keen interest in exploring potential areas of collaboration with the Global Shapers Community.

Key Highlights
Skill Hub Proposal — Plan to establish a technology Skill Hub for students under Chattogram City Corporation schools
Meet the Leader — Part of WEF Global Shapers' global flagship initiative connecting youth with city leaders
Youth Development — Mayor shared ongoing initiatives and future plans for youth empowerment in Chattogram
Collaboration Interest — Mayor expressed keen interest in future collaboration with the Global Shapers Community
🌍
World Economic Forum Initiative
Global Shapers Community · Chattogram Hub
A WEF initiative empowering young leaders to address local, regional & global challenges
